page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter Group Size Cost Breakdown: Small to Large

data.meta_description: Marbella yacht charter group size cost breakdown: from €749 for 9 pax to €4,719 for 12 pax. Compare prices per person and find your perfect fit.
data.summary: A clear breakdown of Marbella yacht charter costs by group size, from small speedboats to large superyachts, with exact pricing and per-person comparisons.
key_facts: Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39: €749/2h up to €2,299/8h for 9–11 pax.
Mangusta 80 (all variants): €4,719 minimum 4h, includes free jet ski, for 12 pax.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.
Smaller boats like Dubhe (5 pax) or Mariah SX21 (5 pax) – pricing on request via WhatsApp.
Per-person cost drops significantly on larger boats: e.g., Mangusta 80 at 4h = ~€393/pax for 12.
Azimut 58 Flybridge and Ferretti 94 also available for 12 pax – contact for bespoke pricing.
All departures from Puerto Banús, Marbella's superyacht hub.

faq.q: What is the cheapest way to charter a yacht in Marbella for a small group?
faq.a: For groups of up to 5, the Dubhe or Mariah SX21 are budget-friendly options – pricing on request via WhatsApp. For 9–11 pax, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours, including all extras.

faq.q: How many people can fit on a Mangusta 80 in Marbella?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 (and its grey/white variants) accommodates up to 12 passengers. It’s ideal for medium-to-large groups, with a minimum 4-hour charter costing €4,719 and a free jet ski included.

faq.q: Are drinks and food included in the charter price?
faq.a: Yes, every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. A licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%) are also always included – no hidden costs.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht for just 2 hours in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 offer 2-hour charters from €749. For larger yachts like the Mangusta 80, the minimum is 4 hours. Smaller boats have flexible durations – enquire via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What is the cost per person for a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: On the Astondoa 40 at 8 hours (€2,299 for 9 pax), it’s ~€255/pax. On the Mangusta 80 at 4 hours (€4,719 for 12 pax), it’s ~€393/pax. Larger groups and longer charters reduce per-person costs.

faq.q: Do I need to pay extra for fuel or skipper?
faq.a: No, all our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and VAT. You pay the listed price – nothing more. Gratuities for the crew are optional.

faq.q: Which yachts depart from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: All our yachts depart from Puerto Banús, including the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80, and all other vessels in our fleet. Puerto Banús is Marbella’s premier marina.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for a group larger than 12 people?
faq.a: Our largest yachts (e.g., Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Maiora 26m) also take 12 pax. For larger groups, you can charter multiple yachts or contact us via WhatsApp for tailored solutions.

data.body_html:
Planning a Marbella yacht charter group size cost breakdown is the smartest way to match your budget with the right boat. Whether you’re a couple wanting a sunset cruise or a group of 12 ready for a full-day party, we’ve got exact prices – no fluff. Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s superyacht capital, and every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. Below, we break down costs by group size so you can compare per-person rates instantly.

Small groups (up to 5 pax): Budget-friendly options

For intimate groups of up to 5, our smallest boats offer the best value. The Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) and Mariah SX21 (6.4m, 5 pax) are perfect for a quick spin along the coast. Pricing is on request via WhatsApp – simply tell us your preferred duration and we’ll quote you instantly. These boats are ideal for a 2-hour blast to Cabopino or a sunset trip past the Golden Mile. All inclusions (skipper, fuel, drinks) apply, so you won’t face surprises.

Medium groups (6–10 pax): The sweet spot for value

Groups of 6–10 have several excellent options. The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are our most popular medium-size charters. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours and go up to €2,299 for 8 hours. At the full-day rate, that’s just ~€255 per person for 9 guests – a steal for a private yacht with crew, drinks, and snacks. Also in this category: the Fairline Targa 12m (10 pax), Pershing 46 (10 pax), and Lagoon 380 catamaran (10 pax) – all pricing on request.

Large groups (11–12 pax): Maximum capacity, maximum fun

For the full 12-passenger experience, our large fleet shines. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) – available in grey and white variants – is a standout at €4,719 minimum 4 hours, with a free jet ski included. That works out to ~€393 per person for a half-day charter. Other 12-pax options include the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m), Canados 86 (26m), Ferretti 94 (29m), K80 (24m), and Maiora 26m – all with bespoke pricing via WhatsApp. For a full-day blowout, these yachts offer space to lounge, swim, and dine in style off the coast of Estepona or Sotogrande.

Per-person cost comparison table

BoatMax paxDurationTotal priceCost per person
Astondoa 4092h€749~€83
Astondoa 4098h€2,299~€255
Azimut 39118h€2,299~€209
Mangusta 80124h€4,719~€393

Note: Mangusta 80 price includes a free jet ski. All other boats’ pricing is available on request – contact us via WhatsApp for exact quotes.

What’s included in every charter?

Every single booking includes: a licensed skipper, fuel, water + soft drinks + beer + white wine + cava, light snacks, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden fees, no add-ons. You can also request a catered menu or extra water toys – just ask when booking. All departures are from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s iconic marina, with easy access to Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the Marbella coastline.

How to choose the right yacht for your group

Start with your group size and budget. For 5 or fewer, the Dubhe or Mariah SX21 keep costs low. For 9–11, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 offer the best per-person value. For 12, the Mangusta 80 is our most popular choice – the free jet ski is a bonus. If you want a catamaran, the Lagoon 380 is stable and spacious. For luxury, the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86 deliver superyacht vibes. Browse our full fleet to compare specs and book directly.

Tips to reduce your Marbella yacht charter cost

  • Choose a shorter duration: 2-hour charters on the Astondoa 40 start at €749 – perfect for a quick coastal tour.
  • Fill the boat: Splitting the Mangusta 80 among 12 people brings per-person cost to ~€393 for 4 hours.
  • Book on weekdays: While prices are fixed, availability is better – contact us for last-minute deals.
  • Skip extras: Our standard inclusions are generous – you won’t need to add much unless you want premium catering.

Frequently asked questions

What is the cheapest way to charter a yacht in Marbella for a small group?

For groups of up to 5, the Dubhe or Mariah SX21 are budget-friendly options – pricing on request via WhatsApp. For 9–11 pax, the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours, including all extras.

How many people can fit on a Mangusta 80 in Marbella?

The Mangusta 80 (and its grey/white variants) accommodates up to 12 passengers. It’s ideal for medium-to-large groups, with a minimum 4-hour charter costing €4,719 and a free jet ski included.

Are drinks and food included in the charter price?

Yes, every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. A licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%) are also always included – no hidden costs.

Can I charter a yacht for just 2 hours in Marbella?

Yes, the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 offer 2-hour charters from €749. For larger yachts like the Mangusta 80, the minimum is 4 hours. Smaller boats have flexible durations – enquire via WhatsApp.

What is the cost per person for a yacht charter in Marbella?

On the Astondoa 40 at 8 hours (€2,299 for 9 pax), it’s ~€255/pax. On the Mangusta 80 at 4 hours (€4,719 for 12 pax), it’s ~€393/pax. Larger groups and longer charters reduce per-person costs.

Do I need to pay extra for fuel or skipper?

No, all our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and VAT. You pay the listed price – nothing more. Gratuities for the crew are optional.

Which yachts depart from Puerto Banús?

All our yachts depart from Puerto Banús, including the Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80, and all other vessels in our fleet. Puerto Banús is Marbella’s premier marina.

How do I book a yacht for a group larger than 12 people?

Our largest yachts (e.g., Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Maiora 26m) also take 12 pax. For larger groups, you can charter multiple yachts or contact us via WhatsApp for tailored solutions.

Ready to book? View our full fleet and prices or send us a WhatsApp message for a custom quote. We’ll help you find the perfect yacht for your group size and budget.
